summary: Iteration 254 — verified the NEW POST /api/accounting/transactions/bulk/direction-override endpoint. 17 new tests across 5 phases (bulk credit/debit flip, bulk 'auto' clear-override+recompute, validation 400s + auth 401/403, tenant isolation, regression of single PATCH/reclassify/list/flow-summary/category-summary/export/diagnostic/sync). All 17 NEW iter-254 tests PASS + all 91 prior iter-248..253 regression tests still PASS = 108/108 PASS.

bulk_credit_debit: POST /bulk/direction-override {ids:[3 seeded ids], direction:'credit'} -> 200 {ok:true, updated:3, direction:'credit'}; all 3 docs have direction='credit' AND direction_override='credit'.
Repeat with 'debit' on the same rows -> updated:3; both fields flip to 'debit' on every row.
bulk_auto_clears_override: Seed: direction='debit', direction_override='credit', raw={transaction_type:'expense', amount:500}. POST {ids:[id], direction:'auto'} -> 200 {ok, direction:'auto'}. Re-fetch shows direction='debit' (recomputed via _direction_of since expense ∈ _DEBIT_TYPES) AND direction_override is GONE (field $unset, not present in returned doc).
validation: 501 ids, direction='credit' -> 400 'Maximum 500 transactions per bulk call'
tenant_isolation: Seed row under tenant_id='OTHER-TENANT'. POST from nyla-air-water admin with that id -> 200 {updated:0}. Foreign row's direction is unchanged AND no direction_override was added — proves the tenant_id filter is enforced in update_many.
regression: Set override='credit' via BULK endpoint, then POST /reclassify-direction -> override is RESPECTED (row stays 'credit', not flipped to 'debit').
All 8 iter-248 + 14 iter-249 + 12 iter-250 + 11 iter-251 + 21 iter-252 + 25 iter-253 prior tests still PASS (91/91).

critical_code_review_comments: BulkDirectionOverrideIn (lines 415-417 of routes/accounting_transactions.py) — minimal Pydantic body, ids:List[str] + direction:str. Clean.

bulk_direction_override (lines 420-467) — _require_admin gate, lowercase+strip normalisation, 400s for invalid direction / empty ids / >500 ids in the correct order. Tenant scoping is correctly applied to BOTH the find() (auto branch) and the update_many() (credit/debit branch) via {tenant_id: tenant_id} — verified by the OTHER-TENANT isolation test. ✓

'auto' branch uses bulk_write of UpdateOne with $set new direction + $unset direction_override per-row after reading raw — correct because each row needs its own recomputed direction. Returns res.modified_count which can legitimately be 0 if only the $unset took effect with no actual direction change; the endpoint documents this implicitly via the re-fetch-to-verify pattern used in the test. ✓
'credit'/'debit' branch is a single update_many — efficient and atomic. Returns modified_count. ✓

MINOR (carried from prior iterations): /app/backend/routes/accounting_transactions.py is now 1027 lines — well past the 700-line guideline. Future refactor candidate (sync / list / tagging / proofs / diagnostic / override sub-modules).
MINOR: The 500-id cap is hard-coded in the endpoint. If frontend ever needs >500, it must page in batches client-side. The error message is explicit so this is fine for now.

OBSERVATION: The 'auto' branch's response.updated reports MongoDB's modified_count, which (per the request notes) can be 0 even when $unset succeeded if the direction value did not change. Test validates persistence by re-fetching the doc — same pattern the frontend should adopt rather than trusting `updated` alone for the auto case.
test_credentials: surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth / test123 (CEO, tenant nyla-air-water). X-Tenant-ID header required.
seed_data_creation: Per-class autouse fixtures seed TEST-ITER254-* docs into accounting_transactions (via pymongo sync client) and clean them up between classes. The tenant-isolation test seeds a row under tenant_id='OTHER-TENANT' to prove the update_many's tenant filter is enforced. All test data deleted on teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 254 adds POST /api/accounting/transactions/bulk/direction-override with body {ids:List[str], direction:'credit'|'debit'|'auto'}. Admin-gated. Caps at 500 ids/call. 'credit'/'debit' uses update_many; 'auto' uses bulk_write of UpdateOne per-row with $set direction + $unset direction_override (because each row's recomputed direction depends on its stored raw payload). Tenant isolation is enforced via {tenant_id} filter on BOTH branches. modified_count can be 0 on the 'auto' branch when only the $unset happened — verify by re-fetch instead of trusting the response counter. The override field (direction_override) is RESPECTED by /reclassify-direction and the background /sync — set by either the single PATCH endpoint (iter 253) or the new bulk endpoint (iter 254). When writing new tests, keep using pymongo sync for direct DB seed/read — the project's motor instance is single-event-loop bound and hangs across pytest's loop boundary.
rca_of_the_issue: Not a bug — feature addition. The bulk endpoint was added because the iter-253 single-PATCH workflow forced users to click each row individually in the Accounting Transactions inbox to fix mis-classified Zoho transfers. The new endpoint lets the admin tick N rows + click one of 3 toolbar buttons (Flip to Money IN / Money OUT / Clear override) and update all of them in a single API call. Implementation is clean, tenant-scoped, admin-gated, and validated; no defects found in 17 targeted tests + 91 regression tests.
